edges.evidence_quote: By negotiating with the Cali Cartel, García Ábrego was able to secure 50% of the shipment out of Colombia as payment for delivery
edges.context: Gulf Cartel negotiated with Cali Cartel for cocaine shipments in the 1980s-1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Zetas, as they are known, served as the hired private mercenary army of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: Los Zetas were the armed wing of the Gulf Cartel from 1997 until a rupture in 2010.

edges.evidence_quote: internal struggles led to a rupture between the Gulf and the Zetas.
edges.context: After Cárdenas' arrest, the Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas became rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: One of the first missions of Los Zetas was to eradicate Los Chachos, a group of drug traffickers under the orders of the Milenio Cartel, who disputed the drug corridors of Tamaulipas with the Gulf Cartel in 2003.
edges.context: Milenio Cartel disputed drug corridors with the Gulf Cartel in 2003.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Chachos... had decided to betray the Gulf Cartel and switch his alliance with the Tijuana Cartel
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel allied with a group that betrayed the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas fought off the Sinaloa Cartel and Los Negros.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas fought against Sinaloa Cartel and Los Negros in Nuevo Laredo.

edges.evidence_quote: the rival Sinaloa Cartel established a heavily armed, well trained enforcer group known as Los Negros.
edges.context: Los Negros were the enforcer group of the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Medellín Cartel (defunct)
edges.context: Medellín Cartel was an ally of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Los Mexicles
edges.context: Los Mexicles are allies of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Camorra
edges.context: Camorra is an ally of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta
edges.context: 'Ndrangheta is an ally of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Serbian mafia
edges.context: Serbian mafia is an ally of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Jalisco New Generation Cartel
edges.context: Jalisco New Generation Cartel is an ally of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Juárez Cartel
edges.context: Juárez Cartel is a rival of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Guadalajara Cartel (defunct)
edges.context: Guadalajara Cartel was a rival of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: La Familia Michoacana
edges.context: La Familia Michoacana is a rival of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Beltrán-Leyva Cartel
edges.context: Beltrán-Leyva Cartel is a rival of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cártel del Noreste
edges.context: Cártel del Noreste is a rival of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Zetas separated from the Gulf Cartel to ally with Beltrán-Leyva Cartel, which led to conflict between them.
edges.context: Los Zetas split from the Gulf Cartel and became rivals, leading to a war.

edges.evidence_quote: Reports from the PGR and El Universal state that while in prison, Cárdenas and Benjamín Arellano Félix, from the Tijuana Cartel, formed an alliance.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel leader Osiel Cárdenas formed an alliance with Tijuana Cartel leader while in prison.

edges.evidence_quote: the Gulf organization joined forces with its former rivals, the Sinaloa Cartel and La Familia Michoacana, aiming to take out Los Zetas.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel allied with Sinaloa Cartel to fight Los Zetas.

edges.evidence_quote: the Gulf organization joined forces with its former rivals, the Sinaloa Cartel and La Familia Michoacana, aiming to take out Los Zetas.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel allied with La Familia Michoacana to fight Los Zetas.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Zetas separated from the Gulf Cartel to ally with Beltrán-Leyva Cartel, which led to conflict between them.
edges.context: Los Zetas allied with Beltrán-Leyva Cartel after splitting from Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Zetas allied with the Juárez Cartel, the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel and the Tijuana Cartel.
edges.context: Los Zetas allied with Juárez Cartel during the conflict with Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Zetas allied with the Juárez Cartel, the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel and the Tijuana Cartel.
edges.context: Los Zetas allied with Tijuana Cartel during the conflict with Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Escorpiones became the armed wing of the entire Gulf organization.
edges.context: Los Escorpiones served as the armed wing of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Costilla Sánchez became the co-leader of the Gulf Cartel and head of the Metros, one of the two factions within the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: Metros is a faction within the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Mario Cárdenas Guillén, brother of Osiel and Antonio, is the other leader of Gulf Cartel and head of the Rojos, the other faction within the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: Rojos is a faction within the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: When the Gulf cartel and Los Zetas split in early 2010, some members of the Rojos stayed with the Gulf cartel, while others decided to leave and join the forces of Los Zetas.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas split in early 2010, becoming enemies.

edges.evidence_quote: The rumors of the broken alliance between the Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas began on blogs and mass emails in September 2009, but it remained pretty much the same throughout that year—a rumor.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas were formerly allied before their split.

edges.evidence_quote: The infighting between the Metros and the Rojos of the Gulf cartel began in 2010
edges.context: Rojos is a faction within the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The infighting between the Metros and the Rojos of the Gulf cartel began in 2010
edges.context: Metros is a faction within the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the Lobos (The wolves) were established in Laredo.
edges.context: Lobos is a faction within the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The infighting between the Metros and the Rojos of the Gulf cartel began in 2010
edges.context: Rojos and Metros are rival factions within the Gulf Cartel, engaged in infighting.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gulf Cartel is believed to have ties with the 'Ndrangheta , an organized crime group in Italy that also has ties with Los Zetas.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel has ties with 'Ndrangheta, including using BlackBerry to communicate.

edges.evidence_quote: The complexity and territorial advantage of Los Zetas forced the Gulf Cartel to seek for an alliance with the Sinaloa Cartel and La Familia Michoacana
edges.context: Gulf Cartel allied with Sinaloa Cartel to fight Los Zetas.

edges.evidence_quote: The complexity and territorial advantage of Los Zetas forced the Gulf Cartel to seek for an alliance with the Sinaloa Cartel and La Familia Michoacana
edges.context: Gulf Cartel allied with La Familia Michoacana to fight Los Zetas.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Zetas joined forces with the Beltrán Leyva Cartel and the Tijuana Cartel to counterattack the opposing cartels.
edges.context: Los Zetas allied with Beltrán Leyva Cartel to fight Gulf Cartel and its allies.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Zetas joined forces with the Beltrán Leyva Cartel and the Tijuana Cartel to counterattack the opposing cartels.
edges.context: Los Zetas allied with Tijuana Cartel to fight Gulf Cartel and its allies.

edges.evidence_quote: On 10 November 2014, a document from the Mexican government was released to the media and claimed that Los Rojos faction of the Gulf Cartel was planning to ally with Los Zetas.
edges.context: Rojos faction planned to ally with Los Zetas to end the war.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Pelones emerged as an independent cartel during this fragmentation as well.
edges.context: Los Pelones emerged from fragmentation of Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: While in prison, Cárdenas and Arellano formed an alliance to defend themselves from the Sinaloa and Juarez Cartels... After a dispute, however, Cárdenas ordered Arellano beaten, and the Gulf-Tijuana alliance ceased to exist at that point.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Tijuana Cartel formed an alliance that later dissolved after a dispute.

edges.evidence_quote: Cárdenas ordered Los Zetas to Baja California to wipe out the Tijuana Cartel.
edges.context: After the alliance ended, Gulf Cartel ordered Los Zetas to attack Tijuana Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the other faction integrated by the Gulf Cartel, Sinaloa Cartel, La Familia Cartel (now extinct) and the Knights Templar Cartel.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Sinaloa Cartel are aligned in the same faction since February 2010.

edges.evidence_quote: the major cartels have aligned in two factions, one integrated by the Juárez Cartel, Tijuana Cartel, Los Zetas and the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel; the other faction integrated by the Gulf Cartel, Sinaloa Cartel...
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Juarez Cartel are in opposing factions, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The rupture from Los Zetas left Jorge Eduardo Costilla Sánchez and Antonio Cárdenas Guillén in full control of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas split, becoming rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: the Gulf Cartel, along with their rival group Los Zetas, have been the two drug cartels with the most kidnappings in all of Mexico
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas are described as rival groups.

edges.evidence_quote: the other faction integrated by the Gulf Cartel, Sinaloa Cartel, La Familia Cartel (now extinct) and the Knights Templar Cartel.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and La Familia Cartel are aligned in the same faction.

edges.evidence_quote: the other faction integrated by the Gulf Cartel, Sinaloa Cartel, La Familia Cartel (now extinct) and the Knights Templar Cartel.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel and Knights Templar Cartel are aligned in the same faction.

edges.evidence_quote: La Maña gang, an alleged sub-group of the criminal group Los Zetas and the Gulf Cartel
edges.context: La Maña is a sub-group of both Los Zetas and Gulf Cartel, indicating a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: a cell within the Gulf Cartel known as Los Flacos
edges.context: Los Flacos is a cell within the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Jesús Enrique Rejón Aguilar, a top-tier Los Zetas boss, was caught on 3 July 2011, and claimed in an interview that was aired on national television that the Gulf Cartel, unlike Los Zetas, has an 'easier and quicker access to arms in the United States'
edges.context: The text contrasts Gulf Cartel and Los Zetas regarding arms access, implying a rivalry or comparison but not a direct relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican drug cartels that operate in the area are currently employing gang members to distribute drugs and conduct other criminal activities on their behalf. Among these gangs... are the Texas Syndicate
edges.context: Gulf Cartel employs Texas Syndicate for drug distribution.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican drug cartels that operate in the area are currently employing gang members to distribute drugs and conduct other criminal activities on their behalf. Among these gangs... are the Latin Kings
edges.context: Gulf Cartel employs Latin Kings for drug distribution.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican drug cartels that operate in the area are currently employing gang members to distribute drugs and conduct other criminal activities on their behalf. Among these gangs... are the Mexican Mafia
edges.context: Gulf Cartel employs Mexican Mafia for drug distribution.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican drug cartels that operate in the area are currently employing gang members to distribute drugs and conduct other criminal activities on their behalf. Among these gangs... are Puro Tango Blast (Vallucos)
edges.context: Gulf Cartel employs Puro Tango Blast for drug distribution.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican drug cartels that operate in the area are currently employing gang members to distribute drugs and conduct other criminal activities on their behalf. Among these gangs... are the Hermandad de Pistoleros Latinos
edges.context: Gulf Cartel employs Hermandad de Pistoleros Latinos for drug distribution.

edges.evidence_quote: The Mexican drug cartels that operate in the area are currently employing gang members to distribute drugs and conduct other criminal activities on their behalf. Among these gangs... are the Tri-City Bombers
edges.context: Gulf Cartel employs Tri-City Bombers for drug distribution.

edges.evidence_quote: In March 2023, a splinter faction from the Gulf Cartel called the Scorpions Group, headed by La Kena, publicly accepted responsibility for an incident where four Americans were kidnapped
edges.context: Scorpions Group is a splinter faction of the Gulf Cartel.
